meta.title: Code Execution & Runtimes
meta.description: The runtime is the foundational platform decision. Master the tradeoffs between server-side containers, browser-based execution, serverless and edge runtimes, and sandboxing mechanisms.

blocks.content:
## Code Execution & Runtimes

*The hardest invisible problem in the stack*

The runtime is the foundational platform decision. Master the tradeoffs between server-side containers, browser-based execution, serverless and edge runtimes, and sandboxing mechanisms.

## Modules in This Level

### 2.1 What a Runtime Actually Is

You write `const x = 2 + 2`. Something has to actually do that addition. That something is the **runtime**.

### 2.2 Server-Side Containers (Docker)

Docker became the default because it solved a real, persistent problem: "it works on my machine." Before containers, deploying software meant managing operating system dependencies, runtime versions, 

### 2.3 Browser-Based Execution (WebContainers)

StackBlitz's WebContainers technology is one of the more impressive pieces of systems engineering in the modern web tooling ecosystem. Understanding how it works is essential for any PM evaluating pre

### 2.4 Serverless Functions & Edge Runtimes

The term "serverless" is a marketing euphemism that obscures a genuinely important architectural shift. The server exists. What's different is who manages it, how it's billed, and — most importantly f

### 2.5 Sandboxing and Isolation

When you run a platform that executes AI-generated code, you are operating on a threat model equivalent to public code execution infrastructure. The code is untrusted. Not because your users are malic

### 2.6 Cold Starts, Warm Pools, and Latency Budgets

Latency is not a single number. It's a pipeline, and each stage has a budget. The PM's job is to understand that pipeline well enough to know where investment buys the most user-visible improvement — 

### 2.7 Framework Support as a Platform Decision
